title: Acystopteris japonica
intro: Acystopteris japonica, also known as Japanese lady fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, offering potential health benefits such as relieving pain and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Acystopteris japonica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Acystopteris japonica is often prepared as a tea, capsule, or extract, and is used to treat various conditions, including fever, sore throat, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Acystopteris japonica is a species of fern, classified under the family Matoniaceae. It is further categorized as a member of the genus Acystopteris. Acystopteris japonica is often grouped with other similar species of ferns that share similar traits and features.
